Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the West Virginia Board of Licensed Dietitians (WVBOLD) under the Independent State Board. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$69
$69 initial application fee
Renewal
$69
Annual + 20 CE hours every two years
Processing Time
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Credential Categories

West Virginia Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice and provide medical nutrition therapy in West Virginia, regulated by WVBOLD.

Provisional Dietitian Permit

Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid while awaiting examination and allows practice under supervision. Costs $46.

Application Process

How to Apply for a West Virginia Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,000 hours of board-approved supervised dietetic experience.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies this.

Submit the Application to WVBOLD

Download and complete the application form from the WVBOLD website. Submit it with the $69 fee.

Provide Verification Documents

Submit a copy of your active CDR card or have verification sent directly to the board. Ensure you meet the age (18+) and citizenship or legal alien requirements.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official ApplicationApplication form filled out accurately and completely, confirming age and citizenship.
  • CDR Registration CardProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (may be satisfied by CDR card, check with board).
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

West Virginia offers a provisional permit for $46 for individuals who possess a degree and have completed the supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. This permit allows the applicant to practice under the supervision of a licensed dietitian while preparing for the exam.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Out-of-state applicants with active CDR registration or a current license in another state with equivalent standards may apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of their active credential/license.

Frequently Asked Questions

West Virginia Dietitian FAQ

How much does a West Virginia dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $69. A provisional permit application costs $46.

When does the West Virginia license need to be renewed?

West Virginia dietitian licenses are renewed annually. The renewal fee is $69.

What are the continuing education requirements for West Virginia?

To maintain licensure, West Virginia requires 20 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) every two years.
